title: Return Values
subtitle: Send data back from a function to the place that called it
learning_objectives: Use the return statement to send a value back from a function
Understand that a function without return gives back None
Store a function's return value in a variable
Explain that return immediately ends the function
Return multiple values as a tuple

sections.heading: The return Statement
sections.content:
So far our functions have printed results, but printing is not the same as returning a value. The return statement sends a value back to the caller, so it can be stored, used in calculations, or passed to another function.

A function that has no return statement (or just return with no value) automatically returns None.
sections.code_example:
def add(a, b):
    return a + b

result = add(3, 4)
print(result)        # 7
print(add(10, 5))    # 15 — used directly

sections.heading: return Ends the Function Immediately
sections.content: When Python reaches a return statement it exits the function right away — any code after return inside that function is never executed. This is called an early return and is often used to handle special cases at the top of a function.

sections.heading: Returning Multiple Values
sections.content: Python lets you return more than one value by separating them with commas. Python packs them into a tuple automatically. You can unpack the tuple into separate variables on the calling side.

exercises.title: Return the Sum
exercises.prompt: Define a function called add that takes two parameters a and b and returns their sum. Then print the result of calling add(3, 4).
exercises.starter_code:
# Define add(a, b) that returns a + b
# Print add(3, 4)
hints: Use return a + b inside the function — not print

exercises.title: Square a Number
exercises.prompt: Define a function called square that takes one parameter n and returns n squared. Print the result of square(5).
exercises.starter_code:
# Define square(n) that returns n squared
# Print square(5)
hints: Remember to use return, not print, inside the function

quiz.question: What does a function return if it has no return statement?
quiz.explanation: In Python, every function returns a value. If you do not write a return statement, Python automatically returns None — a special object meaning 'no value'.

quiz.question: What happens to code written after a return statement inside the same function?
options: It runs after the function is called again
quiz.explanation: return immediately exits the function. Any lines that come after it inside the same function body are unreachable and will never run.

completion_message: Well done! Your functions can now produce values that the rest of your program can use. The return statement is one of the most important tools in a Python programmer's toolkit.
